title: Common
description: Shared primitive types for the insect.eval.v1 contract. Identifiers, timestamps, durations, money, and content-hashes are defined once here and `$ref`-ed everywhere else.

Ulid.title: Ulid
Ulid.description: Crockford-base32 ULID: 26 chars, lexicographically sortable, monotonic when generated within the same millisecond.

Timestamp.title: Timestamp
Timestamp.description: RFC3339 / ISO8601 timestamp with timezone offset, e.g. `2026-05-22T18:30:00Z`. Always normalized to UTC by writers.

Duration.title: Duration
Duration.description: Either an ISO8601 duration string (`PT1H30M`) or a structured `{kind: "millis", value: integer}` envelope. The structured form is preferred when computed from a counter; the string form is preferred when authored by a human.

Money.title: Money
Money.description: Currency-typed monetary value stored as integer micros (1e-6 of the major unit) to avoid floating-point drift in budget accounting.

micros.description: Amount in micros of the major unit. 1_000_000 micros = 1 USD. May be negative for credits/refunds.

ContentHash.title: ContentHash
ContentHash.description: Algorithm-prefixed content hash. Currently only `sha256:` is supported; future algorithms (`blake3:`) MAY be added but receivers MUST reject unknown prefixes.

SemVer.title: SemVer
SemVer.description: Semantic version string. Matches `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` with optional pre-release / build metadata per semver.org.

Url.title: Url
Url.description: Absolute URL. Used for endpoint references, attachment locations, and provenance pointers.

Severity.title: Severity
Severity.description: Ordered severity scale shared by Finding and policy configuration. `blocking` is reserved for issues that must hard-fail CI.

TokenAccounting.title: TokenAccounting
TokenAccounting.description: Per-call or per-trial token usage broken down into input/output and cache tiers, mirroring the cost-and-token model from research/11 §4.

reasoning_tokens.description: Hidden reasoning tokens reported by reasoning-capable models (o-series, Nemotron `detailed thinking`).

Ref.title: Ref
Ref.description: A reference to another entity. Either an inline `{id}` pointer or a fully qualified `$id` URL. Codegen treats this as `Either<Ulid, Url>`.
